zh.lifeSummary: 當Thomas Clarke於1523年出生在英國肯特萊坦時，他的父親Sir James Clarke38歲，母親Joane Agborrowe48歲。他於1548年7月3日與Fuggle在英國英格蘭肯特騰特爾登結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Thomas Clarke于1523年出生在英国肯特萊坦时，他的父亲Sir James Clarke38岁，母亲Joane Agborrowe48岁。他于1548年7月3日与Fuggle在英国英格兰肯特騰特爾登结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。

nameMeanings.name: Clarke
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Irish: variant of   Clark  .

History: This name was brought independently to New England by many bearers from the 17th century onward. Nicholas Clarke was one of the founders of Hartford, CT (coming from Cambridge, MA with Thomas Hooker) in 1635.

variants.name: Clark
meanings: English: from Middle English clerk, clark ‘clerk, cleric, writer’ (Old French clerc; see Clerc ). The original sense was ‘man in a religious order, cleric, clergyman’. As all writing and secretarial work in medieval Christian Europe was normally done by members of the clergy, the term clerk came to mean ‘scholar, secretary, recorder, or penman’ as well as ‘cleric’. As a surname, it was particularly common for one who had taken only minor holy orders. In medieval Christian Europe, clergy in minor orders were permitted to marry and so found families; thus the surname could become established.
Irish (Westmeath, Mayo): in Ireland the English surname was frequently adopted, partly by translation for Ó Cléirigh; see Cleary .
Americanized form of Dutch   De Klerk   or Flemish   De Clerck   or of variants of these names, and possibly also of French   Clerc  . Compare   Clerk   2 and   De Clark  .

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: When Thomas Clarke was born in 1523, in Wrotham, Kent, England, his father, Sir James Clarke, was 38 and his mother, Joane Agborrowe, was 48. He married Fuggle on 3 July 1548, in Tenterden, Kent, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Thomas Clarke est né en 1523, à Wrotham, Kent, Angleterre, son père, Sir James Clarke, avait 38 ans et sa mère, Joane Agborrowe, avait 48 ans. Il a épousé Fuggle le 3 juillet 1548, à Tenterden, Kent, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils.

de.lifeSummary: Als Thomas Clarke 1523 in Wrotham, Kent, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir James Clarke 38 und seine Mutter Joane Agborrowe 48. Er hat am 3. Juli 1548 in Tenterden, Kent, England, United Kingdom Fuggle geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Thomas Clarke è nato il nel 1523 nel Wrotham, Kent, England, il suo padre, Sir James Clarke, aveva 38 anni e il suo madre, Joane Agborrowe, aveva 48 anni. Lui sposato Fuggle il 3 luglio 1548, nel Tenterden, Kent, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio.

ko.lifeSummary: Thomas Clarke(이)가 1523년 영국 켄트 로섬에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir James Clarke(은)는 38세였고, 그의 어머니 Joane Agborrowe(은)는 48세였다. 그는 1548년 7월 3일 영국 잉글랜드 켄트 텐터든에서 Fuggle(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Thomas Clarke nasceu em 1523, em Wrotham, Kent, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir James Clarke, tinha 38 anos e sua mãe, Joane Agborrowe, tinha 48 anos. Ele casou-se com Fuggle em 3 de julho de 1548, em Tenterden, Kent, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Thomas Clarke родился в 1523 г. в местности ротам, кэнт, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir James Clarke было 38 лет, а матери, которую звали Joane Agborrowe, 48. Он женился на женщине по имени Fuggle 3 июля 1548 г. в местности тентирден, кэнт, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Thomas Clarke nació en 1523, en Wrotham, Kent, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir James Clarke, tenía 38 años y su mamá, Joane Agborrowe, tenía 48 años. Se casó con Fuggle el 3 de julio de 1548, en Tenterden, Kent, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo.
